Title: Introduction to the Microsoft Azure Well-Architected Framework
Description: Learn how using key principles throughout your cloud architecture can help you design and build a solid architectural foundation that you can continuously improve.

Title: Load data into a Microsoft Fabric data warehouse
Description: Learn how to load data into a Microsoft Fabric warehouse using data pipelines, T-SQL, and Dataflow Gen2.

Title: Configure Azure Virtual Network peering
Description: Learn to configure an Azure Virtual Network peering connection and address transit and connectivity concerns.

Title: Move forward with Windows 11 Pro post Windows 10 end of support
Description: In this module, you learn how to respond now that Windows 10 support is ended and move forward securely with Windows 11 Pro. Discover why upgrading now is essential, explore the productivity and security benefits of Windows 11 Pro, and find tools to simplify deployment.

Title: Design solutions for security operations
Description: You learn techniques to design security operations capabilities including logging, auditing, Security Information, and Event Management (SIEM), Security Orchestration and Automated Response (SOAR), and security workflows.

Title: Train and evaluate classification models
Description: Train and evaluate classification models

Title: Create, customize, and manage an HPC cluster in Azure with Azure CycleCloud
Description: Learn how to use Azure CycleCloud to create, customize, and manage high-performance computing (HPC) clusters in Azure.

Title: Evaluate and optimize AI agents through structured experiments
Description: Learn how to evaluate and optimize AI agents systematically through structured experiments that measure quality, cost, and performance. Design evaluation metrics, apply Git-based workflows, create consistent scoring rubrics, and make evidence-based optimization decisions.

Title: Create and manage Insider Risk Management policies
Description: Create and manage Insider Risk Management policies.

Title: Apply governance controls to AI-ready workloads
Description: Discover classify AI assets enforce Azure Policy guardrails track data lineage and implement hands‑on governance controls securing compliant auditable AI deployments across infrastructure pipelines and sensitive data workflows.

Title: Operate hybrid infrastructure with Azure Local
Description: Learn how to manage and operate hybrid infrastructure using Azure Local with familiar tools like Windows Admin Center, PowerShell, and Azure Arc.

Title: Implement Microsoft Fabric Data Agents (chat with your data)
Description: Implement Microsoft Fabric Data Agents (chat with your data)

Title: Configure AI Gateway security in Microsoft Foundry
Description: Secure AI model traffic in Microsoft Foundry by deploying and configuring AI Gateway. Examine AI Gateway architecture, create and configure a gateway instance, and apply access controls and monitoring to protect AI workloads.

Title: Implement data security and compliance with SQL
Description: Learn how to implement data security and compliance features in SQL Server, Azure SQL, and SQL databases in Microsoft Fabric, including encryption, masking, Row-Level Security, and auditing.

Title: Migrate very large databases (VLDB) to Azure for SAP
Description: This module explores migration of databases over 20 TB, considered very large databases, and the required techniques and procedures to achieve a migration from on-premises to Azure within an acceptable downtime and with minimized risk. Prepare for Exam AZ-120 Planning and Administering Microsoft Azure for SAP Workloads.

Title: The path to revenue
Description: Learn about the most common startup revenue models. See practical tips on how to get your startup to start generating revenue.

Title: Connect with data stores using PowerShell providers
Description: This module covers PowerShell providers that connect Windows PowerShell to data stores. They offer an easier-to-understand and consistent interface for working with data stores.

Title: Introduction to Azure Bot Service and Bot Framework Composer
Description: Introduction to Azure Bot Service and Bot Framework Composer

Title: Configure Azure Container Registry for container app deployments
Description: Learn how to create and configure an Azure Container Registry, the process of pushing container images to Azure Container Registry and explore different authentication methods and security features for Azure Container Registry.

Title: Monitor your hybrid and multicloud machines through Azure Arc-enabled servers
Description: Understand the monitoring capabilities of Azure Arc-enabled servers. Learn about the benefits of and how to onboard Azure Arc-enabled servers to Azure Logs and Metrics, VM Insights, and Azure Monitor Alerts.

Title: Accessibility evaluation and testing
Description: Explore types of accessibility testing, how to evaluate and test for accessibility, and how to identify and address accessibility issues.

Title: Communicate effectively on GitHub using Markdown
Description: Learn to use Markdown to communicate with brevity, clarity, and expression.

Title: Explore concepts of data analytics
Description: This content is a part of Explore concepts of data analytics.

Title: Use PowerShell drives in PowerShell
Description: This module explains how to work with PowerShell drives.

Title: Explore Azure Pipelines
Description: Learn how Azure Pipelines helps you automate building, testing, and deploying your applications.

Title: Protect your on-premises infrastructure from disasters with Azure Site Recovery
Description: Learn how to provide disaster recovery for your on-premises infrastructure by using Azure Site Recovery to manage and orchestrate replication. Use Site Recovery to perform failover and failback of VMware virtual machines, Hyper-V virtual machines, and physical servers.

Title: Guided project - Calculate and print student grades
Description: Learn to develop an application that will calculate and display results based on given data.

Title: Understand how Azure NetApp Files provides volumes as a service
Description: Understand network attached storage (NAS) concepts and various NAS protocols supported in Azure NetApp Files.

Title: Create Azure Resources Using Azure CLI
Description: Learn how to install and use Azure CLI to create and manage Azure resources interactively and through Bash scripts. Learn CLI syntax, authentication, resource management, and troubleshooting for efficient cloud automation.

Title: Hotpatch updates for Windows client devices
Description: In this module, you’ll explore how Windows hotpatching operates within the broader update ecosystem. You’ll review the configuration steps for enabling hotpatch updates, examine eligibility requirements for x64 and ARM64 devices, and see how ring deployment helps IT admins manage updates safely and efficiently.

Title: Sort and filter results in T-SQL
Description: Sort and filter results in T-SQL

Title: Solutions and benefits for using Azure NetApp Files
Description: Learn about applications for Azure NetApp Files with Linux OSS applications and databases, Windows applications and SQL Server solutions, SAP on Azure solutions, and other products.

Title: Create speech-enabled apps with Azure Speech in Microsoft Foundry Tools
Description: Create speech-enabled apps with Azure Speech in Microsoft Foundry Tools.

Title: Introduction to open source database migration to Azure Cosmos DB
Description: Module on the use of Azure Cosmos DB as part of a migration solution.

Title: Turn insight into action by combining SAP and other data
Description: Explore Microsoft solutions and products that can help you address analytics needs that include both SAP and other data. You’ll learn about using the Azure Synapse Analytics platform to combine data from multiple sources and examine how Power Platform is used to display and analyze business data to turn insights into action.

Title: Introduction to Cluster Shared Volumes
Description: Introduction to Cluster Shared Volumes

Title: Manage network service settings for Windows devices using PowerShell cmdlets
Description: This module covers the PowerShell modules and cmdlets that are used to configure network settings for Windows devices.

Title: Describe significant government strategy actions
Description: Understand legislation, memoranda, executive orders, and policies that significantly set the government's cloud computing approach.

Title: Guided Project – Deploy and configure Azure Monitor
Description: In this module, you practice configuring monitoring of various workloads and infrastructure services using Azure Monitor. The skills validated include deploying and configuring Log Analytics, configuring monitoring for Web apps, monitoring compute and networking services, and configuring alerts.

Title: Plan and implement updates, backups, and disaster recovery
Description: Plan for disaster recovery.

Title: Use AI for everyday tasks
Description: In this module, you dive into the fascinating world of generative AI and discover how it can simplify your daily tasks. You learn the essentials of crafting effective prompts, explore various AI tools, and understand how to analyze and build on AI-generated results. By the end, you'll be equipped with the knowledge to use AI responsibly and ethically, making this an exciting journey for any AI enthusiast!

Title: Manage Windows Server settings using PowerShell cmdlets
Description: This module covers cmdlets that are used to configure settings related to Group Policy, Server Manager, Hyper-V, and Internet Information Services (IIS).

Title: Get started with GitHub Copilot
Description: This module introduces developers to the GitHub Copilot products, the benefits that GitHub Copilot provides to developers, the GitHub Copilot and GitHub Copilot Chat product features, and the GitHub Copilot extensions for Visual Studio Code.

Title: Optimize multi-agent performance and cost in Microsoft Foundry
Description: Optimize multi-agent performance and cost in Microsoft Foundry. Design model routing strategies across agent ecosystems, implement multi-level caching architectures, optimize token usage and context management across agent chains, and systematically analyze quality-cost-latency trade-offs.

Title: Null safety in C#
Description: Learn coding practices to help prevent the occurrence of NullReferenceException.

Title: Plan for cloud workload protections using Microsoft Defender for Cloud
Description: Plan for cloud workload protections using Microsoft Defender for Cloud

Title: Create and manage events
Description: Discover how events facilitate communication between objects in C#, and how their integration with delegates creates robust, decoupled code.

Title: Managing Surface support as a service provider or partner
Description: In this module, you'll learn how to manage Surface support as a service provider or partner. You'll understand the different partner access models for Surface support and how to use a customer's tenant or a partner tenant to manage Surface support. You'll also learn how to manage multiple customer tenants safely and understand partner boundaries and escalation paths.

Title: Build reusable components with Blazor
Description: Learn how to use Razor class libraries to package and deploy components with Blazor.

Title: Manage Microsoft Defender for Endpoint
Description: This module explores using Microsoft Defender for Endpoint to provide additional protection and monitor devices against threats.

Title: Deploy Windows Server
Description: Deploy Windows Server

Title: Manage email efficiently with Microsoft Outlook and Copilot Chat
Description: In this module, you’ll explore how to work more productively with Microsoft Outlook and Copilot Chat.

Title: Identify threats with Behavioral Analytics
Description: Identify threats with Behavioral Analytics

Title: Design authentication and authorization solutions
Description: Azure Architects design and recommend authentication and authorization solutions.

Title: Describe the security management capabilities in Azure
Description: Describe the security management capabilities in Azure.

Title: Begin your Microsoft Learn for Educators Journey
Description: Joining Microsoft Learn for Educators is the start of a journey helping ensure educators feel confident in technical skills related to teaching Microsoft technical content and integrate Microsoft official content into curriculum or syllabus.

Title: Build your first end-to-end test with Playwright
Description: In this module, you'll learn how to use Playwright to test a sample web application. You'll learn how to run tests, view test reports, and understand the structure of a Playwright project. You'll also learn how to use Visual Studio Code to run tests, debug tests, and record new tests.

Title: Enable eye tracking and voice commands for objects on the HoloLens 2
Description: This course provides the user an overall understanding on how to use eye tracking and voice commands

Title: Resolve GitHub issues using GitHub Copilot Agent
Description: This module covers how to identify and remediate GitHub issues using GitHub Copilot's Ask and Agent modes. The training focuses on security vulnerabilities such as SQL injection, encryption, logging, and path traversal problems.

Title: Manage single and multiple computers by using Windows PowerShell remoting
Description: This module explains how to use remoting to perform administration on remote computers.

Title: Integrate with identity management systems
Description: Master identity integration architectures including GitHub SSO, Microsoft Entra ID workload identities, service principals, and managed identities for secure DevOps authentication.

Title: Upload data to Azure Data Lake Storage
Description: Learn about creating an Azure Data Lake Storage account and uploading data to Data Lake Storage Gen 2 through the Azure Storage Explorer.

Title: Identify SAP-certified configurations
Description: Help identify prerequisites, deployment options, and SAP-certified configurations available to you when deploying SAP products in Azure. Prepare for Exam AZ-120 Planning and Administering Microsoft Azure for SAP Workloads.

Title: Create and manage sensitive information types
Description: Create and manage sensitive information types.

Title: Try Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL
Description: Try Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL

Title: Explore fundamentals of Azure Cosmos DB
Description: Explore the features and capabilities of Azure Cosmos DB, Identify supported APIs, and interact with a Cosmos DB database.

Title: Introduction to Azure Copilot
Description: Learn how to use Azure Copilot to perform operations tasks, manage access, and enhance productivity.

Title: Introduction to Microsoft Entra ID
Description: In this module, you’ll learn how to manage identities and access using Microsoft Entra ID.

Title: Secure at every layer with Microsoft Surface
Description: Learn how Microsoft Surface devices are designed with security at every layer, from hardware to firmware to software, providing robust protection for your data and privacy.

Title: Understand the Transformer architecture and explore large language models in Azure Machine Learning
Description: Learn about the history of natural language processing (NLP), including how the Transformer architecture revolutionized the field and helped us create large language models (LLMs). Work with LLMs in Azure Machine Learning through the foundation models in the model catalog.

Title: Configure Microsoft Defender Vulnerability Management settings for Azure VMs
Description: Configure Microsoft Defender Vulnerability Management (MDVM) for Azure VMs by selecting scanning methods, enabling assessment at subscription and machine scope, managing findings with disable rules. Then you can apply Plan 2 premium capabilities including security baselines assessment and application blocking.

Title: Learn the basics of web accessibility
Description: This module covers various concepts on the topic of accessibility and how to add it to your web applications during web development. You will learn the importance of using HTML tags properly to describe information, and various improvements you can make to your sites.

Title: Implement CI/CD pipelines for multi-agent systems with GitHub Actions
Description: Implement enterprise CI/CD pipelines for multi-agent systems using GitHub Actions and Microsoft Foundry. Design coordinated deployment pipelines with agent version compatibility validation, implement canary and blue-green deployment strategies, configure multi-environment strategies, and automate rollback triggered by quality regression metrics.

Title: Understand write-ahead logging
Description: Azure Database for PostgreSQL is an ACID compliant database service. Write-ahead logging ensures changes are both atomic and durable (the A and D in ACID). Changes are first written to the log before they're committed to the database. In this module, you learn how Azure Database for PostgreSQL implements write-ahead logging, and how the log can be used for replication and logical decoding.

Title: Teleport entangled qubits with Q#
Description: Teleport entangled qubits with Q#

Title: Introduction to mixed reality
Description: Learn about core concepts of augmented reality, virtual reality, and mixed reality. Learn about HoloLens and Windows mixed reality immersive headsets.

Title: Implement and manage Microsoft 365 retention and recovery
Description: Implement and manage Microsoft 365 retention and recovery.

Title: Keep Microsoft 365 secure after Windows 10 end of support
Description: In this module, you'll learn how Windows 10 end of support affects Microsoft 365 apps, what it means to stay compliant, and how to plan a smooth transition to Windows 11 or Windows 365 Cloud PCs.

Title: Introduction to cost management for AI workloads
Description: Learn about cloud cost management principles, how AI architecture design decisions impact cost, and how different AI services incur charges.

Title: Troubleshoot Active Directory
Description: Learn how to troubleshoot AD DS service failures or degraded performance. Learn how to recover deleted security objects and the AD DS database, and how to troubleshoot hybrid authentication issues.

Title: Implement observability in a cloud-native .NET 8 application with OpenTelemetry
Description: Learn about observability and how to implement it in a cloud-native application. Use OpenTelemetry packages to output logs, metrics, and tracing data and analyze the data in Application Insights and third-party applications.

Title: Get started with the MongoDB API in Azure Cosmos DB
Description: Learn Azure Cosmos DB for MongoDB basics.

Title: Choose the best Office productivity option for stable and evolving workflows
Description: In this module, you’ll learn how to choose the Office productivity option that best fits your needs by evaluating key decision criteria—such as product model, change tolerance, service dependency, AI benefits, and deployment style—and applying them to real-world scenarios involving stable and evolving workflows.

Title: Describe network-based threats and mitigations
Description: Describe basic networking concepts and how they relate to cybersecurity.

Title: Preprocess data with Data Wrangler in Microsoft Fabric
Description: Explore the process of preparing data for machine learning models using the Data Wrangler tool in Microsoft Fabric notebooks.

Title: Plan Agile with GitHub Projects and Azure Boards
Description: Plan Agile with GitHub Projects and Azure Boards

Title: Configure the Azure Enhanced Monitoring Extension for SAP
Description: This module covers the configuration of the Azure Enhanced Monitoring Extension for SAP. Prepare for Exam AZ-120 Planning and Administering Microsoft Azure for SAP Workloads.

Title: Manage variables in Windows PowerShell scripts
Description: This module explains how to create variables, name them correctly, and assign the correct data type, while ensuring the data you store in variables is in the correct format and easily accessible.

Title: Call methods from the .NET Class Library using C#
Description: Use functionality in the .NET Class Library by calling methods that return values, accept input parameters, and more.

Title: Implement Azure Container Apps
Description: Run serverless containerized apps in Azure Container Apps service.

Title: Optimize costs with Azure NetApp Files
Description: This module looks at cost models and cost optimization features in Azure NetApp Files.

Title: Describe the business benefits of Azure
Description: This module explores various business benefits of Azure, including cost-efficiency, innovation, agility, and security.

Title: Get started with GraphQL in Microsoft Fabric
Description: Learn how GraphQL in Microsoft Fabric works, the key concepts, and practical examples to help users integrate their applications with GraphQL effectively as part of their solutions.

Title: Describe cost management in Azure
Description: Describe cost management in Azure

Title: Describe cloud service types
Description: Introduction to cloud service types (Infrastructure, Platform, and Software as a Service models) and associated use cases for each.

Title: Interactive case study: Modernizing identity and data security
Description: Apply your cybersecurity architect skills on a real business scenario in the area of identity and data security. Analyze design requirements, answer conceptual and technical questions and design a solution to meet the business needs.

Title: Describe the function and identity types of Microsoft Entra ID
Description: Describe the function and identity types of Microsoft Entra ID

Title: Design a solution for backup and disaster recovery
Description: You learn how to select appropriate backup solutions for Azure workloads. You also learn how to select appropriate disaster recovery options for these workloads.

Title: Transform data using notebooks in Microsoft Fabric
Description: Use Fabric notebooks to transform data with Spark SQL and PySpark, connecting to lakehouses, warehouses, and other data stores.

Title: Review CIM and WMI
Description: This module covers CIM and WMI technologies to connect to a common information repository that contains management information that you can query and manipulate. The repository contains all kinds of information about a computer system or device, including hardware, software, hardware drivers, components, roles, services, user settings, and just about every configurable item and the current state of that item. An understanding of the framework and syntax of CIM and WMI will help you know and control almost every aspect of an operating system environment.
